text.title: Django Services Layer Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the django-services methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Service functions are named entity_action (e.g. order_create, user_deactivate). The convention is set project-wide and documented in AGENTS.md.

rationale.source: HackSoft Django Styleguide; internal — refactor audit.

rule.rationale: entity_action makes grep -r 'order_' the canonical impact analysis tool; subject_verb scatters across files.

rule.statement: All service arguments are keyword-only (leading `*`) with full type hints; the return type is hinted. TYPE_CHECKING is used for model types.
rule.rationale: Keyword-only prevents positional mistakes when signatures grow; type hints let pyright catch signature drift.

rule.statement: Apply @transaction.atomic only when the service performs ≥2 writes that must succeed together. Do not wrap read-only or single-write services.
rule.rationale: Unnecessary atomic blocks hold DB locks longer and nest savepoints with surprising rollback semantics.

rule.statement: Side effects that must not run on rollback (Celery dispatch, webhook, email, cache invalidation) use transaction.on_commit.

rationale.source: Django docs; internal — payment incident Q4 2025.

rule.rationale: .delay() or webhook fire inside @atomic can execute before commit completes; rollback then leaves a fired side effect with no row.

rule.statement: Writes live in services; reads with complex permission/policy logic live in selectors. Views call one or the other, never both inline.
rule.rationale: Mixing reads and writes in views replicates the fat-view problem the service layer is supposed to fix.

rule.statement: Inputs are validated by a DRF serializer (or pydantic at the boundary). Services receive validated python types, not raw request data.
rule.rationale: Re-validating inside services duplicates rules and lets unsafe paths skip validation.

rule.statement: If the change is a single-model CRUD with no cross-model logic OR is read-only OR is a derived property, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Forcing the service layer on trivial code adds ceremony with no payback.
